---
title: "Demo_3_PopulationStratification_2025"
author: "Cris Van Hout"
date: '`r Sys.Date()`'
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=TRUE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)

# Load relevant libraries
require(ggplot2)

#Define parameter values for two possibly distinct populations

p1=.5 
mean1=0 
sd1=1 
effect1=0 
numObs1=500 
numObs2=500 
p2=.9 
mean2=1
effect2=0
sd2=1 

# Note that as sample sizes go up, so does sensitivity to differences in effect size and allele frequency

hwprops1=as.integer(as.character(numObs1*(c(p1^2,2*p1*(1-p1),(1-p1)^2))))  #this is still screwy for low MAF and low number of participants
hwprops2=as.integer(as.character(numObs2*(c(p2^2,2*p2*(1-p2),(1-p2)^2))))

geno1<-c(rep(0,hwprops1[1]),rep(1,hwprops1[2]),rep(2,numObs1-hwprops1[1]-hwprops1[2]))
pheno1<-rnorm(numObs1,sd=1,mean=(mean1+effect1*geno1))
geno2<-c(rep(0,hwprops2[1]),rep(1,hwprops2[2]),rep(2,numObs2-hwprops2[1]-hwprops2[2]))
pheno2<-rnorm(numObs2,sd=sd2,mean=(mean2+effect2*geno2))
genoall<-c(geno1,geno2)
phenoall<-c(pheno1,pheno2)
xlim=range(genoall)
ylim=range(phenoall)
ancestry<-c(rep("Blue",numObs1),rep("Red",numObs2))


#consider the effect of the genotype in population 1

titlept1=paste("Pop1: n=",numObs1,"f(a)=",(1-p1),"mean=",mean1,"effect=",effect1)
plot(jitter(geno1,.3),pheno1,xlim=xlim,ylim=ylim,xlab="genotype",ylab="phenotype",col=rgb(0,0,255,150,maxColorValue=255), pch=16,xaxt="n")
mtext(titlept1, side = 3, line = 2, cex = 1, col = "blue")
labels=c("AA","Aa","aa")
axis(1,at=c(0,1,2),labels=labels)

lm1<-lm(pheno1~geno1)
sum.lm1<-summary(lm1)
pval1<-print(sum.lm1$coefficients[2,4],digits=2)
beta1<-sum.lm1$coefficients[2,1]
abline(lm1$coefficients[1],lm1$coefficients[2],col="blue")
label1<-paste("B=",round(beta1,2),"p=",round(pval1,5))
text(1.5, y = ((lm1$coefficients[1]+1.5*lm1$coefficients[2])-.5), labels = label1,col="blue")

#consider the effect of the genotype in population 2
titlept2=paste("Pop2: n=",numObs2,"f(a)=",(1-p2),"mean=",mean2,"effect=",effect2)
plot(jitter(geno2,.3),pheno2,xlim=xlim,ylim=ylim,xlab="genotype",ylab="phenotype",col=rgb(255,0,0,150,maxColorValue=255), pch=16,xaxt="n")
mtext(titlept1, side = 3, line = 2, cex = 1, col = "red")
labels=c("AA","Aa","aa")
axis(1,at=c(0,1,2),labels=labels)

lm2<-lm(pheno2~geno2)
sum.lm2<-summary(lm2)
pval2<-print(sum.lm2$coefficients[2,4],digits=2)
beta2<-sum.lm2$coefficients[2,1]
abline(lm2$coefficients[1],lm2$coefficients[2],col="red")
label2<-paste("B=",round(beta2,2),"p=",round(pval2,5))
text(1.5, y = ((lm2$coefficients[1]+1.5*lm2$coefficients[2])-.5), labels = label2,col="red")

#consider the effect of the genotype in population 1 and 2 jointly, ignoring any difference
plot(jitter(geno1,.3),pheno1,xlim=xlim,ylim=ylim,xlab="genotype",ylab="phenotype",col=rgb(0,0,0,150,maxColorValue=255), pch=16,xaxt="n")
labels=c("AA","Aa","aa")
axis(1,at=c(0,1,2),labels=labels)
points(jitter(geno2,.3),pheno2,col=rgb(0,0,0,150,maxColorValue=255), pch=16)

lmall<-lm(phenoall~genoall)
sum.lmall<-summary(lmall)
pvalall<-print(sum.lmall$coefficients[2,4],digits=6)
betaall<-sum.lmall$coefficients[2,1]


abline(lmall$coefficients[1],lmall$coefficients[2])
labelall<-paste("B=",round(betaall,2),"p=",round(pvalall,6))
text(1.5, y = ((lmall$coefficients[1]+1.5*lmall$coefficients[2])+.25), labels = labelall)


#consider the effect of the genotype in population 1 and 2 jointly, ignoring any difference but plot the populations in different colors

titlept1=paste("Pop1: n=",numObs1,"f(a)=",(1-p1),"mean=",mean1,"effect=",effect1)
titlept2=paste("Pop2: n=",numObs2,"f(a)=",(1-p2),"mean=",mean2,"effect=",effect2)
title12=c(titlept1,titlept2)
plot(jitter(geno1,.3),pheno1,xlim=xlim,ylim=ylim,xlab="genotype",ylab="phenotype",col=rgb(150,0,0,150,maxColorValue=255), pch=16,xaxt="n")
mtext(titlept1, side = 3, line = 2, cex = 1, col = "blue")
labels=c("AA","Aa","aa")
axis(1,at=c(0,1,2),labels=labels)

points(jitter(geno2,.3),pheno2,col=rgb(0,0,150,150,maxColorValue=255), pch=16)
mtext(titlept2, side = 3, line = 1, cex = 1, col = "red")

#consider the effect of the genotype in population 1 and 2 jointly, accounting for difference
titlept1=paste("Pop1: n=",numObs1,"f(a)=",(1-p1),"mean=",mean1,"effect=",effect1)
titlept2=paste("Pop2: n=",numObs2,"f(a)=",(1-p2),"mean=",mean2,"effect=",effect2)
title12=c(titlept1,titlept2)
plot(jitter(geno1,.3),pheno1,xlim=xlim,ylim=ylim,xlab="genotype",ylab="phenotype",col=rgb(150,0,0,150,maxColorValue=255), pch=16,xaxt="n")
mtext(titlept1, side = 3, line = 2, cex = 1, col = "blue")
labels=c("AA","Aa","aa")
axis(1,at=c(0,1,2),labels=labels)

points(jitter(geno2,.3),pheno2,col=rgb(0,0,150,150,maxColorValue=255), pch=16)
mtext(titlept2, side = 3, line = 1, cex = 1, col = "red")

lmall<-lm(phenoall~genoall)
sum.lmall<-summary(lmall)
pvalall<-print(sum.lmall$coefficients[2,4],digits=6)
betaall<-sum.lmall$coefficients[2,1]

lm1<-lm(pheno1~geno1)
sum.lm1<-summary(lm1)
pval1<-print(sum.lm1$coefficients[2,4],digits=3)
beta1<-sum.lm1$coefficients[2,1]

lm2<-lm(pheno2~geno2)
sum.lm2<-summary(lm2)
pval2<-print(sum.lm2$coefficients[2,4],digits=3)
beta2<-sum.lm2$coefficients[2,1]

abline(lmall$coefficients[1],lmall$coefficients[2])
labelall<-paste("B=",round(betaall,2),"p=",round(pvalall,5))
text(1.5, y = ((lmall$coefficients[1]+1.5*lmall$coefficients[2])+.5), labels = labelall)

abline(lm1$coefficients[1],lm1$coefficients[2],col="blue")
label1<-paste("B=",round(beta1,2),"p=",round(pval1,5))
text(1.5, y = ((lm1$coefficients[1]+1.5*lm1$coefficients[2])+1.5), labels = label1,col="blue")

abline(lm2$coefficients[1],lm2$coefficients[2],col="red")
label2<-paste("B=",round(beta2,2),"p=",round(pval2,5))
text(1.5, y = ((lm2$coefficients[1]+1.5*lm2$coefficients[2])-1.5), labels = label2,col="red")


#Ok, now that we see the issue. Lets check a model that resolves the problem

lm1<-lm(pheno1~geno1)
summary(lm1)

lm2<-lm(pheno2~geno2)
summary(lm2)

lmall<-lm(phenoall~genoall)
summary(lmall)

lmallA<-lm(phenoall~genoall+ancestry)
summary(lmallA)

#Summary: It's is not the genotype that is associated with the outcome, it is the ummodeled difference in ancestry.

```
